_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national cron
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize statewide-exec batch: MS (Watson SoS, Reeves Gov) + MT (Knudsen AG, Gianforte Gov, Juras Lt Gov). State tier. Every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence.

profile.religion: Methodist (Galloway Memorial United Methodist Church, Jackson)

tate-reeves.notes_set: 2026 evidence pass (state tier). Term-limited (cannot run 2027). Strong documented social-conservative record: signed REAP Act (trans-minor care ban), Fairness Act (trans sports), school bathroom ban, HB1126 age-verification, anti-gun-registry bill, SHIELD Act (voter citizenship verification), SB2358 (ballot-harvesting ban), HB1365 (Zuckerbucks ban); opted MS into federal tax-credit scholarship; championed HB1523 religious-liberty/conscience law as Lt. Gov.; sued Biden vaccine mandate. Eliminated state income tax (Build Up Mississippi Act). Pro-life ('life begins at conception').

0.note: Publicly stated he believes life begins at conception.

1.note: Signed restrictions (15-week/trigger) but no documented abolition/equal-protection personhood measure.

2.note: No documented position on embryonic stem cell/IVF discard.

3.note: No documented position on euthanasia/assisted suicide.

4.note: Longtime pro-life governor; no abortion-industry PAC funding documented.

0.note: Championed HB1523, premised on belief marriage is one man-one woman.

1.note: No documented advocacy to ban/oppose same-sex marriage in law.

2.note: Signed REAP Act banning gender transition care for minors; signed Fairness Act (sports) and school bathroom ban.

3.note: No documented position on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: Signed school transgender bathroom ban and trans-sports ban.

0.note: Opted MS into federal tax-credit scholarship; vocal school-choice advocate.

1.note: Signed REAP Act barring gender interventions for minors.

2.note: No documented CRT/SOGI/sex-ed curriculum measure.

3.note: Signed HB1126 age-verification/parental-consent for content harmful to minors.

4.note: Championed HB1523 protecting faith-based adoption refusal of same-sex placement.

0.note: Publicly professes faith; hosted streamed prayer services; posts Scripture.

1.note: Championed HB1523 conscience exemptions for Christian businesses/adoption agencies.

2.note: No documented position on compelled-speech/pronoun mandates.

3.note: No documented action on prayer in public bodies/Sabbath protections.

0.note: No documented CBDC/crypto position.

2.note: Income-tax elimination is a tax cut, not a balanced-budget-amendment position.

4.note: Anti-ESG action in MS came from the Treasurer, not Reeves.

0.note: No documented hand-count/anti-machine position.

1.note: Signed SHIELD Act (annual voter-roll citizenship verification, proof of citizenship); MS has photo voter ID.

2.note: No documented single-day-voting position.

3.note: Signed SB2358 banning third-party ballot harvesting.

4.note: Signed HB1365 (2022) banning private/'Zuckerbucks' election funding.

0.note: Backed law enforcement; expanded Capitol Police; LEO bonuses/benefits.

1.note: No documented cashless-bail/rogue-prosecutor measure (record leans to parole reform).

2.note: No documented victim-restitution measure.

0.note: No documented nullification/anti-commandeering statute.

1.note: Sued Biden COVID vaccine mandate as federal overreach.

2.note: No documented resistance specific to federal guns/life/education action.

3.note: Framed federal mandates as 'tyranny'/overreach, asserting state sovereignty.

4.note: No documented action opposing federal capture of elections/data/land.

0.note: No documented border-barrier/military action (state governor).

1.note: No documented deportation measure (SB2114 reported 'preparing to sign', not confirmed).

2.note: HB538 anti-sanctuary reported 'preparing to sign', signature not confirmed.

0.note: Publicly supports MS permitless/constitutional carry (NRA-ILA thanked him).

1.note: Signed anti-gun-registry bill barring firearm/owner registries.

2.note: No documented NFA/GCA repeal position.

4.note: NRA A+ rating is general; no documented ATF/UN-specific action.

profile.religion: Christian (family members of First Presbyterian Church, Pascagoula; father pastors Liberty Church, Gautier)
profile.background: Attorney (BBA + JD, Univ. of Mississippi). MS State Senate District 51 (Jackson County), 2008-2020. 36th Secretary of State since 2020; re-elected 2023.

michael-watson.notes_append: 2026 evidence pass: confirmed sitting SoS (term to Jan 2028). Announced April 2026 he will not seek a third term as SoS; running for Lieutenant Governor in 2027. Rich self-stated positions via iVoterGuide survey + Senate record.

0.note: No explicit life-at-conception statement (authored Fetal Protection Act; MS Right to Life endorsed, but the specific affirmation undocumented).

1.note: Voted for 15-week and heartbeat bans (restrictions, not abolition).

2.note: No documented embryonic/IVF position.

4.note: MS Right to Life endorsed; pro-life; no abortion-industry funding documented.

0.note: Survey: marriage one man-one woman 'should be promoted'; 'God-ordained, sacred' union.

1.note: No documented advocacy to oppose same-sex marriage in law.

2.note: Survey: 'Strongly Disagree' with adding gender identity as a protected class.

0.note: Survey: 'Strongly Agree' funding follows the student; detailed ESA/charter priority.

1.note: No documented parental-consent-for-minors position.

2.note: Survey: 'Strongly Disagree' with CRT institutional-racism claims.

3.note: Survey: supports laws preventing minor access to pornography.

0.note: Survey faith statement professing Christ as Lord and Judeo-Christian framework.

1.note: Survey: 'Strongly Disagree' businesses should be forced to provide services violating religious beliefs.

2.note: No documented compelled-speech/pronoun position.

3.note: Opposed COVID church closures (religious liberty) but no documented prayer/symbols/Sabbath measure.

0.note: No documented CBDC/crypto policy stance.

2.note: No documented balanced-budget position.

0.note: No documented hand-count/anti-machine position.

1.note: Champions photo voter ID and the SHIELD Act citizenship verification; supports federal SAVE Act.

2.note: Defended MS's absentee grace period in Watson v. RNC (a duty as SoS); no clear single-day-voting policy stance.

4.note: Welcomed HB1365 ban on private/'Zuckerbucks' election funding.

0.note: Survey: 'Strongly Disagree' with defunding police; supports qualified immunity.

1.note: No documented cashless-bail/rogue-prosecutor position.

2.note: No documented victim-restitution position.

0.note: No documented nullification/anti-commandeering vote.

1.note: No documented refusal of a federal mandate/strings.

2.note: No documented resistance specific to federal guns/life/education action.

3.note: Opposed federal 'For the People Act'/EO on registration, asserting state authority over elections.

4.note: Opposed federal takeover of state elections (HR1).

0.note: No documented border-barrier/military position.

1.note: No documented mandatory-deportation position.

2.note: Authored 'comprehensive illegal immigration law' as senator but contents/anti-sanctuary specifics undocumented.

0.note: NRA A-rated and 2A priority, but the specific constitutional-carry citation (campaign page) is dead; not scoring on an unverifiable source.

1.note: No verifiable specific red-flag/registry position (NRA endorsement source unreachable).

2.note: No documented NFA/GCA position.

profile.background: 5th-generation Culbertson farm/ranch family. MT House (HD 34), 2011-2018, Speaker 2015 & 2017; Roosevelt County Attorney 2018-2020. 25th Attorney General since 2021; re-elected 2024 (term to 2029).

austin-knudsen.notes_append: 2026 evidence pass: confirmed sitting AG; Dec 2025 MT Supreme Court dismissed the disciplinary case and declined suspension (no effect on office). Rich litigation/amicus record. NOTE: the 2024 abortion measure he challenged is CI-128 (not CI-14); the Texas 2020 election suit predates his AG term.

0.note: No explicit life-at-conception statement (Dobbs statement framed as returning the issue to the people).

1.note: Anti-abortion litigation (Dobbs amicus, blocked CI-128, mifepristone brief) but no abolition/equal-protection measure.

2.note: No documented embryonic/IVF position.

3.note: No documented euthanasia position (Baxter predates his tenure).

4.note: Filed Dobbs amicus; consistent anti-abortion litigator; no abortion-industry funding documented.

0.note: No documented marriage-definition statement.

1.note: No documented same-sex-marriage position.

2.note: Defending SB99 (minor gender-care ban) and SB458 (defining sex as binary), 'state law that reflects scientific reality.'

4.note: Led amicus briefs opposing schools concealing kids' gender identity from parents; defends SOGI-in-schools limits.

0.note: No documented school-choice/homeschool action.

1.note: Led 18- and 22-state parental-rights briefs on minors' gender info; asked SCOTUS to hear parental-rights case.

2.note: Issued binding AG opinion declaring CRT pedagogy unlawful discrimination.

3.note: Defending MT Age Verification Act (Free Speech Coalition v. Knudsen).

0.note: No documented faith profession/religious affiliation.

1.note: No documented conscience-exemption action.

2.note: Generic 'free speech' coalitions do not map to compelled-speech-against-Christian-conviction.

0.note: No documented CBDC stance (banking actions are anti-debanking/ESG).

2.note: No documented balanced-budget position.

4.note: Strong anti-ESG record: 24-AG letter, BlackRock probe, sued NAAG over ESG, antitrust suit vs BlackRock/Vanguard/State Street.

0.note: No documented hand-count position.

1.note: No documented voter-ID action.

2.note: No documented single-day-voting position.

4.note: No documented Zuckerbucks position (federal-registration suit scored under federal overreach).

0.note: Tough-on-crime AG; funds officers/prosecutors; fentanyl enforcement.

1.note: No documented cashless-bail position.

2.note: Backed HB791 mandatory minimums for fentanyl sales (proportional punishment of evildoers).

0.note: No documented nullification statute.

1.note: Filed/joined three suits against Biden COVID vaccine mandates.

2.note: Resisted federal overreach on guns (ATF pistol-brace suit), life (mifepristone), education (parental rights).

3.note: Asserts state sovereignty against unconstitutional federal action across multiple suits.

4.note: Sued over Biden EO 14019 (federal agencies conducting voter registration).

0.note: No documented border-barrier/military action.

1.note: Joined suits against Biden deportation moratorium/enforcement-priority limits, favoring broader deportation enforcement.

2.note: Demanded Gallatin County rescind a policy not recognizing ICE; anti-sanctuary posture.

0.note: No documented defense of HB102 constitutional carry specifically.

1.note: Sued ATF pistol-brace rule; led amicus vs bump-stock ban; challenged ghost-gun rule.

2.note: No documented NFA/GCA repeal advocacy.

greg-gianforte.notes_append: 2026 evidence pass: confirmed sitting governor (re-elected 2024). Very broad signing record. NOTE: vetoed HB968 (2025) parental-consultation bill on parental-rights grounds (objected to lowering minor age 18->16), not a pro-abortion rationale.

0.note: Pro-life ('boldly defend life') but no explicit life-at-conception/personhood statement (a personhood bill failed in 2025).

1.note: Signed 15-week ban, born-alive, clinic regs (restrictions, not abolition).

2.note: No confirmed embryonic/IVF measure (SB154 fetal-tissue-sale ban not confirmed signed).

4.note: Signed multiple pro-life bills; no abortion-industry funding documented.

0.note: No documented explicit marriage-definition statement.

1.note: No documented same-sex-marriage position.

2.note: Signed SB99 (minor care ban), SB458 (sex binary), HB121 (bathroom), HB112 (sports), SB280 (birth cert).

4.note: Signed HB359 (drag-show/story-hour ban in schools/libraries); urged regents on trans college sports.

0.note: Signed HB562 charter-school law, HB549, and ESAs for students with disabilities.

1.note: Condemned MT Supreme Court ending parental consent for minors' abortion as 'anti-parental rights.'

2.note: No documented CRT/SOGI/sex-ed curriculum bill.

3.note: No source confirming he signed MT's age-verification act (defended by AG; not cited to him).

0.note: Openly evangelical (Grace Bible Church); spoke at National Day of Prayer.

1.note: Signed SB215, Montana Religious Freedom Restoration Act.

2.note: No documented compelled-speech position.

0.note: Signed SB178 'right to mine' protecting decentralized crypto; reported anti-CBDC measure.

2.note: Income-tax cuts are not a balanced-budget-amendment position.

4.note: Signed HB228 anti-ESG and HB356 firearms-industry banking nondiscrimination.

0.note: No documented hand-count position.

1.note: Signed SB276 photo voter ID (2025).

2.note: Eliminated Election Day registration but no single-day-voting/absentee-restriction match.

3.note: Signed HB530 banning paid ballot collection (ballot harvesting).

0.note: Funded corrections pay/public safety; backed law enforcement.

1.note: No documented cashless-bail position.

2.note: Strengthened human-trafficking penalties (proportional punishment of evildoers).

0.note: Signed HB102 barring state/local enforcement of federal gun bans (anti-commandeering).

1.note: Pulled MT out of federal COVID unemployment; signed HB702 vaccine-mandate ban.

2.note: HB102 resists federal gun overreach.

3.note: Called federal vaccine rule 'gross federal overreach'; refused emergency powers.

4.note: No documented action opposing federal capture of elections/data/land.

0.note: Sent MT National Guard to the Texas border (Operation Lone Star).

1.note: No documented mandatory-deportation measure.

2.note: Signed HB200 banning sanctuary cities; investigated Helena.

3.note: 2025 illegal-hiring crackdown not confirmed as an E-Verify mandate.

4.note: Signed foreign-adversary ban on purchasing MT agricultural land.

0.note: Signed HB102 constitutional (permitless) carry.

1.note: Signed HB809 barring local red-flag/extreme-risk protection orders.

2.note: No documented NFA/GCA repeal position.

4.note: HB102 bars enforcement of federal gun bans (opposes federal disarmament).

profile.religion: Christian (denomination undocumented; adviser to UM Christian Legal Society)
profile.background: Attorney ~40 yrs (Great Falls) representing farmers/ranchers/small business; Univ. of Montana law professor (property, contracts, agricultural/water law) from 2000. Ran for MT Supreme Court 2016 (lost). 37th Lt. Governor since 2021; re-elected 2024.

kristen-juras.notes_append: 2026 evidence pass: confirmed sitting Lt. Governor. Lt. governors have thin independent records; scored ONLY her own quoted statements/HARD record (Christian liberty, biological-sex/HB121 testimony), null where only the administration's acts or an undocumented aggregator (powervoter.us) exist.

0.note: No documented life-at-conception statement (pro-life label is from an undocumented aggregator).

1.note: No documented abolition position.

2.note: No documented embryonic/IVF position.

4.note: Defended Christian employers refusing to cover abortion; pro-life-aligned; no abortion-industry funding documented.

0.note: Advised UM Christian Legal Society requiring a statement of faith that marriage is between a man and a woman; stated ministers shouldn't be forced to perform same-sex marriages.

1.note: No documented position opposing same-sex marriage in law (her statement is minister-conscience).

2.note: Testified first for HB121: 'acknowledging biological realities should not be controversial'; affirms biological sex.

4.note: HB121 testimony tied to protecting women's sports and children from gender ideology in policy.

0.note: Read a school-choice proclamation (representative role); no personal quoted endorsement.

2.note: Campus free-speech (HB349) testimony does not specifically map to compelled-speech-against-Christian-conviction.
